meta.title: LangChain Explained
meta.subtitle: The framework that makes AI agents buildable, debuggable, and deployable
meta.author: Imbila.AI

summary: LangChain is an open-source framework for building applications powered by large language models. It provides composable building blocks — chains, RAG, tools, memory, and agents — that snap together through a consistent API. The ecosystem includes LangChain (high-level framework), LangGraph (graph-based orchestration), and LangSmith (observability, evals, deployment). v1.0 stable since November 2025.

products.name: LangChain
products.description: Composable chains, prompts, tools, memory, and output parsers. Supports 20+ LLM providers. Great for straightforward agent builds and prototyping. Available in Python and TypeScript, v1.0 stable.

products.name: LangGraph
products.description: Stateful, graph-based workflows for complex multi-step agents, human-in-the-loop flows, and branching logic. The engine under LangChain's agent layer. Supports cycles, persistence, and controllability.

products.name: LangSmith
products.description: Tracing, experiment comparison, automated evals, and production monitoring. The DevOps layer for AI agents. Includes Fleet for no-code agent building and enterprise deployment.

products.name: Deep Agents
products.description: Async subagents, multi-modal file support, and sandboxed execution. LangChain's production-grade coding and research agents introduced in 2026.

products.name: Fleet
products.description: Formerly Agent Builder. Enterprise hub for building, using, and managing agents with natural language. No-code agent building — describe what you want, it builds the agent.

framework.description: High-level framework with composable chains, prompts, tools, memory, output parsers. 20+ LLM providers. Python and TypeScript.

orchestration.description: Graph-based orchestration for stateful multi-step agents. Cycles, persistence, human-in-the-loop, branching logic.

platform.description: Observe, evaluate, and deploy. Tracing, experiment comparison, automated evals, production monitoring. Includes Fleet for no-code agent building.

use_when: You're building agents with tools and reasoning loops.
You need RAG over your own documents.
You want to swap models without rewriting code.
You need observability and evaluation from day one.
You're orchestrating multi-step workflows with LangGraph.
Your team wants a standard, well-documented framework.
skip_when: A single API call solves your problem.
You're building a simple chatbot with no tools.
You need maximum control over every HTTP request.
Your use case is pure text generation with no orchestration.
You're deeply invested in another framework (CrewAI, AutoGen, etc.).
The abstraction layer adds complexity you don't need.

use_cases.title: Document Q&A
use_cases.description: Internal docs, manuals, legal contracts — employees ask questions in natural language instead of hunting through PDFs. RAG-powered, always grounded in your data.

use_cases.title: Support Agents
use_cases.description: Agents that resolve tickets, look up order status, escalate complex issues, and learn from past resolutions. Tool-using agents, not keyword bots.

use_cases.title: Data Analysis
use_cases.description: Agents that read spreadsheets, query databases, run calculations, and produce reports. Particularly powerful with code execution tools and Deep Agents.

use_cases.title: Coding Assistants
use_cases.description: Code review, bug detection, documentation generation, and automated testing. Open SWE provides the architecture for internal coding agents.

use_cases.title: Process Automation
use_cases.description: Agents that monitor inboxes, classify documents, update CRMs, and trigger downstream actions. Multi-agent systems where each agent owns a step.

use_cases.title: Generation Pipelines
use_cases.description: Marketing teams using chains to generate, review, and refine content — with brand guidelines baked into the prompt template and structured output enforced.

timeline.title: LangChain Launches
timeline.detail: Harrison Chase releases the initial Python library. Chains and prompt templates for LLM apps.

timeline.title: Explosive Growth
timeline.detail: TypeScript support, 700+ integrations, LangSmith beta. Becomes the most-starred AI framework on GitHub.

timeline.title: LangGraph Introduced
timeline.detail: Low-level graph-based orchestration for stateful, multi-step agents. Cycles, persistence, human-in-the-loop.

timeline.title: v1.0 — Stable Release
timeline.detail: LangChain and LangGraph hit v1.0 together. create_agent becomes the canonical entry point. Model profiles, middleware, structured output.

timeline.title: Deep Agents, Fleet & Beyond
timeline.detail: Deep Agents (async subagents, multi-modal), Fleet (enterprise agent management), LangSmith self-hosted expansion. The agent engineering platform emerges.

code_examples.title: Basic Chain (LCEL)
code_examples.code:
from langchain_core.prompts import ChatPromptTemplate
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI

chain = prompt | model | parser
result = chain.invoke({"topic": "AI agents"})

code_examples.title: RAG Pipeline
code_examples.code:
# Load -> Split -> Embed -> Store -> Retrieve
docs = loader.load()
splits = splitter.split_documents(docs)
vectorstore.add_documents(splits)
retriever = vectorstore.as_retriever()

code_examples.title: Tool-Using Agent
Create Agent (v1.0+)
code_examples.code:
from langchain.agents import create_agent

agent = create_agent(
    "anthropic:claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
    tools=[search, calculator, code_exec],
    system_prompt="You are a research assistant..."
)
